jokes.text: Those are some chairs. That's a cat or opossum.
jokes.setup: Tour guide pointing out basic office furniture
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions inability to identify basic animals at Animal Control
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - shows incompetence through casual delivery. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Oh, this is a napkin where I wrote down a cool name for a dog. 'Bark Obama.' Right?
jokes.setup: Showing off department organization
jokes.punchline: Proud display of terrible pun written on napkin
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun but delivered with perfect character pride. Gets points for quotability.

jokes.text: Oh. Yo. Yeah. We found this bird outside. We tried to turn it into a work whistle, like in the beginning of The Flintstones. Tougher than it looked, though.
jokes.setup: Explaining workplace innovation
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of animal abuse through cartoon logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - applying cartoon physics to real animals. Dark humor with perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: We have a massive pest problem in this town, and it's because the Animal Control Department has been disaster... at catching animals. They're great at ingesting and selling marijuana.
jokes.setup: Professional assessment of department performance
jokes.punchline: Reveals their actual expertise is drugs, not animals
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline with good misdirection. Leslie's exasperation comes through perfectly.

jokes.text: This place is a miserable suck hole run by two pot-head losers. It's completely and utterly mismanaged. Thanks!
jokes.setup: Leslie's harsh but accurate assessment
jokes.punchline: Harris cheerfully thanks her for the insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Harris's obliviousness creates great contrast. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: There's more than one way to skin a cat. Four. There's four ways to skin a cat. Regular blade--
jokes.setup: Common idiom about multiple solutions
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation with disturbing specificity
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant subversion of common saying. Shows character's disturbing literalism perfectly.

jokes.text: Coyote trap, dude... Yeah, we got, like, a huge vermin problem. I mean, we've been meaning to call somebody about it, but you know, nobody get on the phone.
jokes.setup: Explaining workplace hazard
jokes.punchline: Not realizing they ARE the people to call about vermin
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Animal Control doesn't understand their own job. Good setup for Ben's realization.

jokes.text: You're the people you would be calling. That is so trippy.
jokes.setup: Ben pointing out obvious irony
jokes.punchline: Brett's mind-blown reaction to basic logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect stoner logic - simple concepts become profound. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: Yo, let's go to Jamaica. No, we should go to Jamaica. Oh, is that what you said? Yeah, dog.
jokes.setup: Trying to make plans after being fired
jokes.punchline: Both suggest identical plan, confusion ensues
jokes.explanation: Shows their mental state perfectly - can't even coordinate identical thoughts.

jokes.text: Usually, I take it neat, but I will make an exception in the name of health.
jokes.setup: Leslie suggesting he drink water for health
jokes.punchline: Ron treats water like alcohol with his drinking terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson - applies drinking culture to basic hydration. Excellent character consistency.

jokes.text: Last night, I watched a movie with Diane and the girls in which an orange fish is separated from his father. The children were sniffling, which I believed to be due to the sad nature of the film. I was wrong.
jokes.setup: Ron describing family movie night and children's emotional response
jokes.punchline: He misread the situation - they were sick, not emotional
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron misreading emotions. His formal description of Finding Nemo is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Please turn the thermostat up to 90 and leave me alone.
jokes.setup: Ron being sick and stubborn about help
jokes.punchline: Extreme temperature request reveals how sick he really is
jokes.explanation: Shows Ron's stubbornness even when clearly very ill. Good escalation of his condition.

jokes.text: Ron collapsing
jokes.setup: Ron insisting he's fine and needs no help
jokes.punchline: Immediately collapses, proving he does need help
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy timing. Perfect punctuation to his stubborn denial.

jokes.text: Uh, 'Spasm.' No, 'Butter Face'! Very good! Yes!
jokes.setup: Pop quiz about cologne scents
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous perfume names that sound like insults
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - cologne names that are accidentally offensive. Shows Andy's enthusiasm.

jokes.text: The man owns a Rolexus. It's a Lexus filled with Rolexes. What's the point of that? To have it, which he does.
jokes.setup: Describing wealthy eccentric's possessions
jokes.punchline: Circular logic - the point is simply to own the ridiculous thing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept with perfect circular logic. 'Rolexus' is inspired wordplay.

jokes.text: I already took what I call a 'dwyer shower.' I rubbed my armpits with air fresheners.
jokes.setup: Leslie telling Andy to shower before meeting
jokes.punchline: Andy's gross alternative to actual hygiene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - creates his own terminology for laziness. Disgusting but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Friend of mine, bit of an alcoholic, a little grabby, either a war hero or war criminal, depending on who you ask, but a good dude. How do you know him? I do his teeth.
jokes.setup: Describing qualifications of candidate
jokes.punchline: Increasingly concerning details followed by mundane dentist relationship
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of red flags followed by mundane connection. Shows Jeremy's poor judgment.

jokes.text: A few coffee stains up front, but his canines are insane. Gosh, Jeremy, he sounds perfect. Are his gums great too? Oh, yeah. They are excellent, beet red.
jokes.setup: Jeremy focusing on dental details instead of qualifications
jokes.punchline: Leslie's sarcastic enthusiasm, Jeremy missing it completely
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Jeremy completely missing Leslie's sarcasm while describing unhealthy gums.

jokes.text: This whole place runs on dibs.
jokes.setup: Explaining government appointment process
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex governance to playground rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect summation of government dysfunction in four words. Excellent economy and truth.

jokes.text: For 'date of birth,' you wrote 'springtime.' Which is true.
jokes.setup: Doctor reviewing Ron's evasive medical form answers
jokes.punchline: Ron's technically correct but useless response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - giving technically true but deliberately unhelpful information. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: How many drinks of alcohol do you consume a week? One. That's it, one drink? One shelf.
jokes.setup: Standard medical question about alcohol consumption
jokes.punchline: Ron measures alcohol by shelving unit, not drinks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection and perfect character moment. 'One shelf' is an inspired measurement unit for Ron.

jokes.text: Do you exercise? Yes. Lovemaking and woodworking.
jokes.setup: Standard health question about exercise
jokes.punchline: Ron's uniquely personal definition of physical activity
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron - reducing exercise to his core interests. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Do you have any history of mental illness in your family? I have an uncle who does yoga.
jokes.setup: Medical question about family mental health history
jokes.punchline: Ron considers yoga a mental illness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron worldview - yoga as mental illness. Excellent character consistency and unexpected perspective.

jokes.text: Allergies? Cowardice and weak-willed men. And hazelnuts.
jokes.setup: Medical question about allergies
jokes.punchline: Philosophical allergies followed by mundane real one
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from abstract to concrete. The hazelnut addition is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Sexual history. Epic and private.
jokes.setup: Medical form question about sexual history
jokes.punchline: Ron's confident but uninformative response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - grandiose but private. 'Epic and private' is excellent character voice.

jokes.text: If I give as much as I get, I'm giving $20 million. Ay yi yi. That's how much I'm worth, 20...million...dollars.
jokes.setup: Tom suggesting charitable giving proportional to wealth
jokes.punchline: Dennis's obnoxious wealth bragging with dramatic pauses
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Dennis as insufferable rich guy. The dramatic pauses add cringe factor.

jokes.text: Ah, he's disgusting, but I want to take his money and give it to needy people, so I'm just gonna keep on smiling. Yay!
jokes.setup: Leslie dealing with Dennis's obnoxious behavior
jokes.punchline: Forced enthusiasm for charitable goals despite personal disgust
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment - her principles forcing her to endure awful people. The 'Yay!' is well-timed.

jokes.text: Harris, we know who you are. I literally just fired you. Oh, is this that job? That's crazy! Never mind.
jokes.setup: Harris arriving for job interview
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's applying for the job he was just fired from
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure showing Harris's complete obliviousness. Well-executed character consistency.

jokes.text: What up? Yo, my name is Brett. I like burgers, and also, I'm very high right now. Dude, this is hilarious! Oh, yo! We live together! Yo, we live in the same place! This is my boy! Hey, 'Wyld Stallyns'!
jokes.setup: Second former employee arriving for interview
jokes.punchline: Stoned reunion and Bill & Ted reference
jokes.explanation: Good stoner character work but relies on familiar tropes. The reunion energy is authentic.

jokes.text: Actually, when I went there, it was just a two-year college. Wow, so you're not even technically qualified to work at your current job. Aw, geez.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining his educational background
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he's not qualified for his current position
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry - accidentally revealing his incompetence. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: I am very, very, very, very frightened of animals.
jokes.setup: Interviewing for animal control position
jokes.punchline: Extreme fear of the things he'd need to control
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup - worst possible candidate trait for the job

jokes.text: You see Dr. Richard Nygard? I'm a Nygardian too!
jokes.setup: Candidate mentioning his therapist
jokes.punchline: April creating weird cult-like term for therapy patients
jokes.explanation: Classic April weirdness, creating false camaraderie through bizarre terminology

jokes.text: I studied zoology in college, and I can control animals with my mind. Get out. I made you say that.
jokes.setup: Orin explaining his qualifications
jokes.punchline: Claiming mind control powers and taking credit for rejection
jokes.explanation: Good Orin character moment - creepy confidence in impossible abilities. Well-executed absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Balsa wood? You could at least use mahogany.
jokes.setup: Doctor using tongue depressor
jokes.punchline: Ron critiquing medical equipment's wood quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - applying woodworking standards to medical equipment. Excellent character consistency.

jokes.text: Wish all patients were like that. It's really annoying when they ask questions.
jokes.setup: Ron leaving abruptly after diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Doctor preferring patients who don't care about their health
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about medical apathy. Shows doctor's unprofessional attitude well.

jokes.text: I think that guy wants to hunt me.
jokes.setup: Dennis describing his hunting preferences
jokes.punchline: Tom realizing he might be the intended prey
jokes.explanation: Good realization moment with excellent economy. Tom's dawning awareness is well-timed.

jokes.text: You're a dick. Excuse me? How dare you talk to me like that? Where are your manners?
jokes.setup: Dennis being consistently rude throughout meeting
jokes.punchline: Dennis outraged at being called out for his own behavior
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Dennis lecturing about manners after being awful. Andy's directness pays off.

jokes.text: I run a children's break dancing studio called 'D.J. Jazzy Vern's.'
jokes.setup: Vern explaining his background
jokes.punchline: Completely unrelated business with dated hip-hop reference
jokes.explanation: Absurd business name and concept, relies somewhat on dated reference

jokes.text: Hey, you know what, he's not a rack of ribs, so stop grilling him. Nailed it.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking Vern questions
jokes.punchline: Jamm's terrible pun about grilling, proud of his bad joke
jokes.explanation: Terrible pun but perfect for Jamm's character, self-satisfaction makes it work

jokes.text: Ribs are better smoked than grilled, so un-nailed it, with the back part of the hammer.
jokes.setup: Someone saying 'nailed it' about grilling
jokes.punchline: Ron's overly specific correction with tool metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good Ron moment - technical correction with specific terminology. The 'back part of the hammer' is nicely detailed.

jokes.text: I don't like lotion. I like my hands to be cracked and calloused like a railway worker. I know. I filled the bottles with fake blood, vinegar, and mud.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving April beauty products
jokes.punchline: Leslie knowing April well enough to customize the gift
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Leslie's thoughtfulness matches April's dark preferences perfectly.

jokes.text: What's your name? Batman. See? I failed.
jokes.setup: Leslie practicing easy confirmation hearing questions
jokes.punchline: April deliberately giving wrong answer to prove her point
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - undermining Leslie's help with deliberate wrong answer. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I will be your Pocahontas. Climb into my papoose.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to convince April to do the hearing
jokes.punchline: Bizarre metaphor mixing movies and baby carriers
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie enthusiasm with confused metaphors. 'Climb into my papoose' is memorably weird.

jokes.text: Those are personal. No one's allowed to read those.
jokes.setup: Council reading April's explicit emails to Andy
jokes.punchline: April's genuine embarrassment about privacy violation
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of April vulnerability, good cringe comedy

jokes.text: But only if you divorce Ben. Hey, don't even joke about that. What? I really think he's wrong for you.
jokes.setup: April agreeing to do the hearing
jokes.punchline: Her genuine disapproval of Leslie's marriage
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - April's protective feelings toward Leslie expressed negatively.

jokes.text: Alcohol is delicious and fun...damentally a substance that should not be abused.
jokes.setup: April answering about underage drinking
jokes.punchline: Leslie secretly providing the end of her sentence
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay and misdirection. The ventriloquist effect creates good visual comedy.

jokes.text: Wow. Ms. Ludgate, are you a ventriloquist? I mean, that is amazing how you make your words come out of Ms. Knope's mouth. Would you like to put her on your knee?
jokes.setup: Jeremy noticing Leslie helping April
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Leslie sit on April's lap like a ventriloquist dummy
jokes.explanation: Good observation turned into visual joke. The image of Leslie on April's knee is amusing.

jokes.text: I guess my first move as director would be to fill your office, house, and car with snakes, and then train a crow to fly up your butt.
jokes.setup: April answering what she'd do as director
jokes.punchline: Elaborate revenge fantasy against councilmen
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - creative, dark, specific revenge fantasy

jokes.text: I guess my first move as director would be to fill your office, house, and car with snakes, and then train a crow to fly up your butt.
jokes.setup: Asked about her plans as director
jokes.punchline: Elaborate revenge fantasy involving animals
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - using animals for revenge instead of animal control. Creative and character-specific threat.

jokes.text: This check is made out to 'Go [bleep] yourself.' In your face! Dennis Feinstein helps no man!
jokes.setup: Dennis appearing to make generous donation
jokes.punchline: Revealing the check is actually an insult
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character consistency. Dennis's pettiness reaches new heights.

jokes.text: Mr. Feinstein, with all due respect... you are a major dick. / Run!
jokes.setup: Ben trying diplomatic approach
jokes.punchline: Direct insult followed by immediate retreat
jokes.explanation: Ben breaking character to be direct, immediate consequences

jokes.text: Eddie, get my crossbow. Crossbow? What the--
jokes.setup: Dennis angry about being called a dick
jokes.punchline: Escalating to medieval weapons in modern office
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and absurdist element. Eddie's confusion adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Think about Ivy and Zoe and Diane, and eat a damn banana.
jokes.setup: Ann trying to get Ron to take care of his health
jokes.punchline: Escalating from deep emotional appeal to mundane fruit consumption
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from emotional to practical, sweet character moment

jokes.text: No, thank you. I live the way I live, I eat the things I eat, and I'll die the way I'll die.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting he eat bananas for health
jokes.punchline: Ron's philosophical rejection of medical advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron philosophy - accepting death rather than eating fruit. Excellent character consistency and structure.

jokes.text: There shouldn't be a separate Animal Control Department. Its budget is tiny, and it's ineffective, and frankly, no one could change that.
jokes.setup: Asked why she deserves the job
jokes.punchline: Argues the job shouldn't exist instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - instead of arguing for herself, she argues against the position. Shows April's intelligence.

jokes.text: Somebody wake up Milton. History is being made. Iwo Jima!
jokes.setup: Chris excited about April's proposal
jokes.punchline: Milton waking up confused, shouting historical reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Milton. The random historical reference works for elderly character.

jokes.text: I can write up an official report in language simple enough for even you dummies to understand, dummies.
jokes.setup: April offering to write official report
jokes.punchline: Insulting the council while offering to help them
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - helpful and insulting simultaneously

jokes.text: Hang on a second. You seem to be forgetting that I already told Vern he got the job, and he already bought a new speedboat.
jokes.setup: Good idea being proposed
jokes.punchline: Jamm's corrupt commitment to bad decision based on speedboat purchase
jokes.explanation: Jamm's ridiculous priorities and corrupt commitment to bad decision

jokes.text: I'm just sick of being on the losing side. I'm going to tell everyone this was my idea. Retro-jammed.
jokes.setup: Jamm switching sides to support April
jokes.punchline: Claiming credit and coining term for retroactive credit-taking
jokes.explanation: Good Jamm character moment. 'Retro-jammed' is a creative addition to his vocabulary.

jokes.text: I'm just sick of being on the losing side. I'm going to tell everyone this was my idea. Retro-jammed.
jokes.setup: Jamm unexpectedly supporting April's idea
jokes.punchline: Immediately planning to take credit and creating new term for it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm opportunism, 'retro-jammed' is a great coined term

jokes.text: Rent-A-Swag is gonna donate five cents from every dollar we make this month.
jokes.setup: Tom offering to donate after Feinstein's rejection
jokes.punchline: Extremely small percentage reveals his business's profitability
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's business struggles through generous gesture.

jokes.text: I had it made your first week here. I knew you'd be going places, so I wanted to be prepared.
jokes.setup: April asking how Chris made plaque so quickly
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been planning this promotion for years
jokes.explanation: Good Chris character moment showing his optimistic planning. Sweet and slightly obsessive.

jokes.text: Can I hire an intern? Not yet. Can I hire a Mexican elf? No. Can I buy a child bride? No. Then how is this even a promotion?
jokes.setup: April asking about her new authority
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate requests leading to logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect April escalation with dark humor. Her logic is twisted but internally consistent.
